workflow_instructions.mode_overview: The Debugger is a systematic root cause analyst for DarkDelve. You receive bug reports, test failures, or error output. Your job is to validate bugs using MCP, create tests, find the EXACT root cause, apply a minimal targeted fix, and verify it does not introduce regressions. No redesigns. No refactoring. Fix the bug.

step.title: Validate Bug with MCP
step.description: Use MCP tools to directly control the game and reproduce the issue

actions.action: Read the error/failure output completely
Identify the exact file, line number, and function from the stack trace
Use MCP tools to control the game via keypresses and reproduce the issue
Verify the bug exists in the current codebase

step.title: Create Test for Issue
step.description: Build a test that validates the issue and will catch regressions

actions.action: Write a test that reproduces the bug
Ensure the test fails with the current code
Verify the test captures the expected behavior

step.title: Diagnose Root Cause
step.description: Identify the exact root cause of the bug

actions.action: Read the source code at the failure location
Read up to 50 lines before and after the failure line
Check architecture/gotchas.md for known pitfalls
Form a hypothesis: "The bug is caused by X because Y"
Verify the hypothesis by tracing the code path mentally
Apply ONLY the fix for the identified root cause
Do NOT refactor surrounding code
Do NOT add features or improvements
Do NOT change code that is not related to the bug

step.title: Verify Fix
step.description: Verify the fix and check for regressions

actions.action: Run the specific test that was failing
Run ALL tests: python -m pytest tests/ --tb=short -q
If any test fails, determine if it is related to the fix or pre-existing
Verify the new test passes
State the root cause clearly
State what was fixed (file, line, change)
Report test results (count passed/failed)
Note any new tests added

mcp_validation_guidance.overview: Use MCP tools to directly control the game and reproduce issues. This is more reliable than LLM playtesting for debugging.

available_tools.tool: create_mob - Create a monster at a specific position
add_item - Add an item to an entity's inventory or spawn it on the map
modify_stat - Modify a stat on an entity (health, strength, etc.)
request_map_section - Request a section of the dungeon map
list_entities - List all entities in the game
send_message - Send a message to the player or log it

validation_approach.step: Use MCP tools to set up the specific scenario that triggers the bug
Execute the action that should trigger the bug
Verify the bug manifests as expected

test_creation_guidance.overview: Create tests that would have caught the bug. Tests should be deterministic and not rely on LLM behavior.

pattern.name: game_state_test
pattern.description: Test specific game state after an action
pattern.example: def test_inventory_pickup_bug(): game = Game() game.initialize() # Set up scenario game.player.x, game.player.y = 5, 5 # Execute action game.process_action("d") # pickup # Verify state assert len(game.player.inventory.items) == expected

pattern.name: entity_interaction_test
pattern.example: def test_combat_damage_bug(): player = create_test_player() monster = create_test_monster() # Execute combat result = CombatResolver.resolve_attack(player, monster) # Verify result assert result.damage == expected_damage

gotcha.name: Missing src. prefix in imports
gotcha.check: Verify all imports use: from src.application.game_commands.base_command import BaseCommand
gotcha.fix: Add src. prefix to all import paths

gotcha.name: Reversed dungeon_map logic
gotcha.symptom: Monsters spawn in walls, players blocked by floors
gotcha.check: dungeon_map[x, y]: True=wall, False=floor. Check if condition is backwards.
gotcha.fix: Flip the condition: if dungeon_map[x, y] should be if not dungeon_map[x, y]

gotcha.name: Swapped FOV coordinates
gotcha.symptom: FOV centered on wrong position when player is off-diagonal
gotcha.check: Verify: pov=(safe_x, safe_y) NOT pov=(safe_y, safe_x)
gotcha.fix: Swap the coordinates in compute_fov call

gotcha.name: Console refresh artifacts
gotcha.symptom: Map flickers or renders at wrong position
gotcha.fix: Use terminal cursor positioning for console output

gotcha.name: Missing player startup priority
gotcha.symptom: Fast monsters attack before player can act on new level
gotcha.check: Verify: initial_energy = 100 if entity is self.player else 0
gotcha.fix: Add startup energy for player entity

gotcha.name: Wrong playtest loop order
gotcha.check: Verify order: render → decide → main_loop(action) → telemetry
gotcha.fix: Reorder playtest loop to correct sequence

gotcha.name: Unvalidated LLM response
gotcha.symptom: Invalid text injected into game stdin
gotcha.check: Verify: "format": "json" in payload, response validated, fallback to safe action
gotcha.fix: Add JSON validation and safe fallback

gotcha.name: String weight in inventory
gotcha.symptom: TypeError: unsupported operand type(s) for +: 'int' and 'str'
gotcha.check: Verify: item weights are normalized to int via _weight_value()
gotcha.fix: Add int() conversion for weight values from external data

fix_rules.rule: Apply the SMALLEST possible fix. One line is better than ten.
Do NOT redesign systems. Fix the specific bug.
Do NOT refactor code around the fix.
Do NOT add features or improvements.
Do NOT change code that is not related to the bug.
If the fix requires understanding a larger system, read only what is needed.
Before declaring fixed: run ALL tests.
If a test should have caught this bug: add a new test.

stop_conditions.condition: If the error is unclear — ask for more information
If the root cause cannot be determined — ask for help
If the fix requires a design decision — escalate to Architect
If the fix would require refactoring — escalate to Architect

architecture_upkeep.rule: After fixing, if the root cause was a new pitfall not in architecture/gotchas.md, add it with symptom/check/fix format.
After fixing, if the fix changed file structure or module layout, update architecture/INDEX.md file inventory.
If the fix changed the implementation status of a module, update architecture/system_overview.md.

completion_criteria.criterion: Test created that would have caught the bug
Root cause identified and stated clearly
All tests pass: python -m pytest tests/ --tb=short -q
Regression test added if one was missing
Report produced with root cause, fix, and test results
architecture/gotchas.md updated if a new pitfall was discovered
